Anya E. Vostinar
ClassesResearchBlogAbout

Web Accessibility Resources

Color blindness

Color blindness simulator for images: upload or drag-and-drop an image and choose from several filters.

Color blind webpage filter: enter a URL and compare side-by-side how people with normal vision and people with various forms of color blindness see the same page.

Daltonization: an algorithm to correct for colors that are hard to detect by red-green color blind people. Note that the “try it” functionality no longer seems to be working.

Color safe palette generator.

Dyslexia

From HuffPost: This website simulates what dyslexia is really like

The dyslexia simulator link at the bottom of that article is broken. Here’s a link to a different simulator.

Parkinson’s disease

A day in the life of a Parkinson’s Disease sufferer. From the site GoodDays.

What it feels like to have Parkinson’s Disease. From Science Friday.

Accessibility tools

Web accessibility evaluation tools. A list maintained by the Web Accessibility Initiative of W3C.

Accessibility options on the Mac / iOS.

Accessibility options on Windows PCs.

Basic Android accessibility settings.

  • AnyaEVostinar

The homepage of Prof. Anya E. Vostinar. I teach computer science and research the evolution of symbiosis using digital evolution and agent-based modeling. I wrote the Symbulation platform.
